reviews.review_description: Game has potential but not enough for the price. Hopefully they will add more to this game because I love the art style.

For what this game is, it’s at least playable. However, there’s a BUNCH of issues.

First off, the post processing is not only bad, but it’s not inputted correctly. When you look at any neon light, it’s like through one lens it’s positioned good but on the other lens you see where the light is supposed to be but the glare is so far away from where it SHOULD be. It’s like when you’re crossing your eyes and you see double of what you’re looking at, and it’s REALLY distracting.

Second of all, in the menu there’s an option to have one handed aim, and two handed aim. Selecting the two handed aim doesn’t do anything, there’s no recoil, the gun doesn’t have any physics, you might as well just act like two handed aim doesn’t exist since it doesn’t do anything.

This game is another wave shooter where you boom everybody to get points. Unfortunately this game just feels so cheap. What I mean is you don’t have the ability to dodge any bullets flying in your direction via the joystick. It’s all stationary. The enemies can just attack your base in order to deal damage. You do have a shield and it works, but if you dodge a bullet you SHOULD be able to not suffer damage if you didn’t get hit. But it doesn’t matter. If it attacked your vicinity, YOU take damage anyway.

My MAIN issue with the gameplay in general is this. You could be killing a lot of enemies and out of nowhere you magically get sent to the next level even when there are still enemies on that floor that are still alive... So clearly I won right? HOW did I win? Did I kill enough enemies? Did I survive long enough? WHO KNOWS? BECAUSE THE GAME NEVER EXPLAINS IT TO ME!

Last and certainly not least, when you die, you get the LAMEST game over screen I have ever seen in a vr game. You basically die but the game freezes and an underwhelming PNG appears that says GAME OVER. No dramatic red lights shining, the room doesn't go dark, NOTHING HAPPENS! It doesn't even feel like you died. It's just like "Wait what happened? Get this crappy PNG out of my way and let me play the game!"

Anyway, this isn't worth 25 dollars. This feels more like 10 dollars at best. I don't care that there are supposedly better classes that I need to unlock in order to get more out of it, this game just left a bad first impression.
Totally worth the $25 I spent on this, it is so fun, I really enjoy all of the different classes and diverse enemies, I have spent so many hours playing this and having fun
